ddls:
CREATE TABLE "devices" (
  "device_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each device */,
  "device_name" TEXT /* Name of the device */,
  "device_type" TEXT /* Type of device (e.g., smartwatch, fitness tracker) */,
  "serial_number" TEXT /* Serial number of the device */,
  "firmware_version" TEXT /* Version of the firmware installed on the device */,
  "manufacturing_date" TEXT /* Date when the device was manufactured */,
  "mac_address" TEXT /* MAC address of the device */,
  "battery_capacity" INTEGER /* Battery capacity of the device in mAh */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("device_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_packets" (
  "packet_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data packet */,
  "device_id" INTEGER /* ID of the device that transmitted the data packet */,
  "local_timestamp" INTEGER /* Local timestamp when the data packet was received */,
  "emotibit_timestamp" INTEGER /* EmotiBit timestamp when the data packet was transmitted */,
  "packet_number" INTEGER /* Packet number of the data packet */,
  "data_length" INTEGER /* Length of the data packet */,
  "type_tag" TEXT /* Type tag of the data packet */,
  "protocol_version" INTEGER /* Protocol version used to transmit the data packet */,
  "data_reliability" INTEGER /* Reliability of the data packet */,
  "sequence_number" INTEGER /* Sequence number of the data packet */,
  "acknowledgement_status" INTEGER /* Acknowledgement status of the data packet */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("packet_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_packets_device_id FOREIGN KEY ("device_id") REFERENCES devices ("device_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_packet_versions" (
  "version_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data packet version */,
  "packet_id" INTEGER /* ID of the data packet that this version belongs to */,
  "version_number" INTEGER /* Version number of the data packet */,
  "version_timestamp" INTEGER /* Timestamp when the data packet version was created */,
  "changes_made" TEXT /* Description of changes made in this version */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("version_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_packet_versions_packet_id FOREIGN KEY ("packet_id") REFERENCES data_packets ("packet_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "physiological_signals" (
  "signal_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each physiological signal */,
  "packet_id" INTEGER /* ID of the data packet that contains the signal */,
  "signal_type" TEXT /* Type of physiological signal (e.g., heart rate, skin conductance) */,
  "signal_value" REAL /* Value of the physiological signal */,
  "unit_of_measurement" TEXT /* Unit of measurement for the physiological signal */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("signal_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_physiological_signals_packet_id FOREIGN KEY ("packet_id") REFERENCES data_packets ("packet_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "activity_levels" (
  "activity_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each activity level */,
  "packet_id" INTEGER /* ID of the data packet that contains the activity level */,
  "activity_type" TEXT /* Type of activity level (e.g., steps, distance) */,
  "activity_value" INTEGER /* Value of the activity level */,
  "unit_of_measurement" TEXT /* Unit of measurement for the activity level */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("activity_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_activity_levels_packet_id FOREIGN KEY ("packet_id") REFERENCES data_packets ("packet_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "signal_annotations" (
  "annotation_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each annotation */,
  "signal_id" INTEGER /* ID of the physiological signal that this annotation belongs to */,
  "annotation_text" TEXT /* Text of the annotation */,
  "annotation_timestamp" INTEGER /* Timestamp when the annotation was made */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("annotation_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_signal_annotations_signal_id FOREIGN KEY ("signal_id") REFERENCES physiological_signals ("signal_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., researcher, data analyst, admin) */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hash of the user's password */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "device_assignments" (
  "assignment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each device assignment */,
  "device_id" INTEGER /* ID of the device being assigned */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user being assigned the device */,
  "assignment_date" TEXT /* Date when the device was assigned */,
  "return_date" TEXT /* Date when the device was returned */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("assignment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_device_assignments_device_id FOREIGN KEY ("device_id") REFERENCES devices ("device_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_device_assignments_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_sessions" (
  "session_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user session */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user that the session belongs to */,
  "session_start" INTEGER /* Timestamp when the session started */,
  "session_end" INTEGER /* Timestamp when the session ended */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("session_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_sessions_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_access_log" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each log entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user that accessed the data */,
  "packet_id" INTEGER /* ID of the data packet that was accessed */,
  "access_timestamp" INTEGER /* Timestamp when the data was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., read, write) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_access_log_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_access_log_packet_id FOREIGN KEY ("packet_id") REFERENCES data_packets ("packet_id")
)
